source_notes: Iron ore (not coking coal) but included for the iron-ore parallel: Mitsui-ITOCHU marketing arrangement directed majority of Mt Newman output to Japanese steel mills via JSM long-term contracts negotiated by Nippon Steel-led consortium. Initial dedication ~90% to Japan; declined to ~60% by 2010 as China rose. Source: Mitsui 50-yr retrospective; BHP 40-yr release.

Same as Mt Newman — JSM iron-ore SPAs since first cargo 1972. Original SPAs with Japanese steel mill consortium for nameplate 15 Mtpa, 15-yr terms with rolling 5-yr extensions. Source: Rio Tinto 50-yr Robe River release.

Inaugural JSM coking-coal SPA when CQCA opened Goonyella 1971; supplied Nippon Steel, Sumitomo Metal, NKK, Kawasaki Steel, Kobe Steel via the JSM consortium. Standard 5-yr SPA terms, perpetually renewed under the Annual Benchmark regime through 2010 (when spot pricing displaced benchmark). Source: NS Energy Business Goonyella Riverside profile; JSM annual coking coal benchmark history.
Same as Goonyella — JSM SPA from first production 1972. Source: Mining Monthly BMA profile.
Same as Goonyella — JSM SPA from first production 1974. Source: Wikipedia Saraji.

Production began 2003; Japanese equity partners (Nippon Steel 8% + Marubeni Coal 6.67% + Sumisho Coal 3.33% = 18%) and dedicated SPAs to JSM. ~80% of initial output to Japan. Source: Wikipedia Hail Creek; Glencore acquisition release 2018.
Thermal (not coking) coal. Mitsui's 10% equity stake bundled with offtake SPA for ~50% of initial output to Japanese power utilities (TEPCO, Chubu, Kansai). Source: Wesfarmers history.

Thermal coal. Mitsubishi Development's 10% equity stake bundled with thermal-coal SPAs to Japanese power utilities at modernized open-cut commencement 1986. ~50% of initial output to Japan. Source: Glencore Ulan page.
